elements.name: follow
elements.brief: Follow a game object.
elements.description: Follow a game object.

parameters.name: options

elements.name: get_zoom
elements.brief: Get the current zoom level of the camera.
elements.description: Get the current zoom level of the camera.

parameters.name: camera_id
parameters.doc: (hash | url), undefined for the first camera

elements.name: set_zoom
elements.brief: Change the zoom level of the camera.
elements.description: Change the zoom level of the camera.

parameters.name: zoom
parameters.doc: The new zoom level of the camera

elements.name: shake
elements.brief: Shake the camera.
elements.description: Shake the camera.

parameters.name: intensity
parameters.doc: Intensity of the shake, in percent of screen. Defaults to 0.05

parameters.name: duration
parameters.doc: Duration of the shake, in seconds. Defaults to 0.5

parameters.name: direction
parameters.doc: Direction of the shake. Possible values: `both`, `horizontal`, `vertical`. Defaults to `both`.
types: { both?: boolean; horizontal?: boolean; vertical?: boolean }

parameters.name: cb
parameters.doc: Function to call when the shake has finished. Optional.

elements.name: stop_shaking
elements.brief: Stop shaking the camera.
elements.description: Stop shaking the camera.

parameters.name: camera_id
parameters.doc: (hash | url), undefined for the first camera

elements.name: screen_to_world
elements.brief: Translate screen coordinates to world coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera.
elements.description: Translate screen coordinates to world coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera.

parameters.name: camera_id
parameters.doc: (hash | url), undefined for the first camera

elements.name: window_to_world
elements.brief: Translate window coordinates to world coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera.
elements.description: Translate window coordinates to world coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera.

parameters.name: camera_id
parameters.doc: (hash | url), undefined for the first camera

elements.name: get_display_size
elements.brief: Get the display size (ie from game.project).
elements.description: Get the display size (ie from game.project).

elements.name: get_window_size
elements.brief: Get window size.
elements.description: Get window size.

elements.name: get_cameras
elements.brief: Get list of camera ids.
elements.description: Get list of camera ids.

elements.name: get_view
elements.brief: Get the current view of the camera.
elements.description: Get the current view of the camera.

elements.name: get_viewport
elements.brief: Get the current viewport of the camera.
elements.description: Get the current viewport of the camera.

elements.name: get_projection
elements.brief: Get the current projection of the camera.
elements.description: Get the current projection of the camera.

elements.name: get_projection_id
elements.brief: Get the current projection id of the camera.
elements.description: Get the current projection id of the camera.

elements.name: recoil
elements.brief: Apply a recoil effect to the camera. The recoil will decay using linear interpolation.
elements.description: Apply a recoil effect to the camera. The recoil will decay using linear interpolation.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.doc: Offset to apply to the camera. Defaults to 0.05

parameters.name: duration
parameters.doc: Duration of the recoil, in seconds. Defaults to 0.5

elements.name: get_offset
elements.brief: Get the current offset of the camera (caused by shake or recoil)
elements.description: Get the current offset of the camera (caused by shake or recoil)

elements.name: follow_offset
elements.brief: Change the camera follow offset.
elements.description: Change the camera follow offset.

elements.name: unfollow
elements.brief: Stop following a game object.
elements.description: Stop following a game object.

elements.name: deadzone
elements.brief: If following a game object this will add a deadzone around the camera position where the camera position will not update. If the target moves to the edge of the deadzone the camera will start to follow until the target returns within the bounds of the deadzone.
elements.description: If following a game object this will add a deadzone around the camera position where the camera position will not update. If the target moves to the edge of the deadzone the camera will start to follow until the target returns within the bounds of the deadzone.

parameters.name: left
parameters.doc: Number of pixels to the left of the camera

parameters.name: top
parameters.doc: Number of pixels above the camera

parameters.name: right
parameters.doc: Number of pixels to the right of the camera

parameters.name: bottom
parameters.doc: Number of pixels below the camera

elements.name: bounds
elements.brief: Limits the camera position to within the specified rectangle.
elements.description: Limits the camera position to within the specified rectangle.

parameters.name: left
parameters.doc: Left edge of the camera bounds

elements.name: screen_to_world_bounds
elements.brief: Translate screen boundaries (corners) to world coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera.
elements.description: Translate screen boundaries (corners) to world coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera.

elements.name: world_to_screen
elements.brief: Translate world coordinates to screen coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera, optionally taking into account an adjust mode. This is useful when manually culling game objects and you need to determine if a world coordinate will be visible or not. It can also be used to position gui nodes on top of game objects.
elements.description: Translate world coordinates to screen coordinates, based on the view and projection of the camera, optionally taking into account an adjust mode. This is useful when manually culling game objects and you need to determine if a world coordinate will be visible or not. It can also be used to position gui nodes on top of game objects.

parameters.name: adjust_mode
parameters.doc: One of gui.ADJUST_FIT, gui.ADJUST_ZOOM and gui.ADJUST_STRETCH, or undefined to not take into account the adjust mode.
types:
| typeof gui.ADJUST_FIT
			| typeof gui.ADJUST_STRETCH
			| typeof gui.ADJUST_ZOOM

elements.name: unproject
elements.brief: Translate screen coordinates to world coordinates using the specified view and projection.
elements.description: Translate screen coordinates to world coordinates using the specified view and projection.

elements.name: project
elements.brief: Translate world coordinates to screen coordinates using the specified view and projection.
elements.description: Translate world coordinates to screen coordinates using the specified view and projection.

elements.name: add_projector
elements.brief: Add a custom projector that can be used by cameras in your project (see configuration above).
elements.description: Add a custom projector that can be used by cameras in your project (see configuration above).

parameters.name: projector_id
parameters.doc: Id of the projector. Used as a value in the projection field of the camera script.

parameters.name: projector_fn
parameters.doc: The function to call when a projection matrix is needed for the camera. The function will receive the id, near_z and far_z values of the camera.
types: (id: Hash, near_z: number, far_z: number) => void

elements.name: use_projector
elements.brief: Set a specific projector for a camera. This must be either one of the predefined projectors (see above) or a custom projector added using `camera.add_projector()`.
elements.description: Set a specific projector for a camera. This must be either one of the predefined projectors (see above) or a custom projector added using `camera.add_projector()`.

elements.name: set_window_scaling_factor
elements.brief: Set window scaling factor (basically retina or no retina screen). There is no built-in way to detect if Defold is running on a retina or non retina screen. This information combined with the High DPI setting in game.project can be used to ensure that the zoom behaves the same way regardless of screen type and High DPI setting. You can use an extension such as DefOS to get the window scaling factor.
elements.description: Set window scaling factor (basically retina or no retina screen). There is no built-in way to detect if Defold is running on a retina or non retina screen. This information combined with the High DPI setting in game.project can be used to ensure that the zoom behaves the same way regardless of screen type and High DPI setting. You can use an extension such as DefOS to get the window scaling factor.

elements.name: PROJECTOR
